description: Letter (bottom part) from Alexandria to Fustat, dictated by Abu l-Najm Hilal, written down by Abu l-Muna, and addressed to Abu Ishaq b. Yaʿaqov at funduq al-Mahalli, who is to give it to Abu l-Majd the cantor—the brother of Hilal. The writer sends greeting to the addressee’s wife and the teacher Yiṣḥaq. The silk that Abu l-Majd sent to Mahasin has arrived. The old woman (presumably the mother of Hilal and Abu l-Majd) is frail and no longer able to work, and everyone is distressed, and they wish Abu l-Majd to come before she dies. The Ḥaver and Ibn Daud reached an arrangement to alternate Shabbats (in receiving income?) starting after Passover. Information in part from Goitein's note card. EMS. ASE.
tags: old age, Alexandria, Illness, illness: furqa, illness: threatening to die, illness: dying, Illness: women's, illness letter 969-1517
description_he: מכתב (חלק תחתון) מאלכסנדריה לפוסטאט, שהכתיב אבו אל-נג'ם הלאל וכתב אבו אל-מונא: אמם הזקנה רפויה ואינה יכולה לעבוד; כולם מצטערים ומבקשים שהאח יגיע לפני מותה. המשי שנשלח הגיע. החבר ואבן דאוד סיכמו על חלוקת שבתות לאחר הפסח.
